documents.text: Company RAG Policy (2024). All customer data must be encrypted at rest and in transit. Access to production retrieval indices requires SSO and MFA. Embeddings are computed using approved third-party APIs; no PII may be included in evaluation logs without redaction.

Data Retention Policy. Raw customer documents are retained for 90 days in hot storage and archived to cold storage for 365 days. After 365 days, data is purged unless a legal hold is active. Derived embeddings follow the same retention window as the source document.

Access Control Policy. Production access to FAISS indices and embedding caches is restricted to members of the on-call rotation. All access is logged and audited quarterly. Break-glass credentials are rotated after each use and require two-person approval.

Architecture note: Hybrid retrieval combines BM25 lexical scoring with dense embeddings. Score fusion uses a configurable alpha to balance precision (sparse) and semantic recall (dense). Reranking is optional and should be gated behind feature flags due to latency and cost.

Embedding layer design. We use OpenAI text-embedding-3-small (1536 dim) by default and sentence-transformers BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 (384 dim) for local deployments. Vectors are L2-normalized before insertion into FAISS IndexFlatIP so inner product equals cosine similarity.

Chunking strategy. The default recursive chunker splits text by paragraph, then sentence, targeting 512 tokens with 64-token overlap. The semantic chunker groups sentences by cosine similarity above a 0.75 threshold; it is slower but yields more topically coherent chunks for long documents.

Embedding cache. Redis is the primary cache with 7-day TTL; the service falls back to an in-memory dictionary when Redis is unavailable. Cache keys are SHA-256 hashes of the input text, which deduplicates identical chunks across documents.

Operations: When confidence scores fall below the configured threshold, the system must return an insufficient-information response or a clarifying question instead of a speculative answer. All query traces must include retrieved chunk identifiers for audit.

Incident response. Pager alerts fire when p95 query latency exceeds 2000 ms for five consecutive minutes, or when the guardrail fallback rate exceeds 20 percent over a 15 minute window. On-call acknowledges within 5 minutes and posts status updates to #rag-incidents every 15 minutes.

Deployment runbook. The service is deployed via blue-green on Kubernetes. Rollouts wait for a readiness probe on /health plus a smoke query against a canary corpus. Rollback is automatic if the error rate on /query exceeds 2 percent within the first 10 minutes.

Evaluation methodology. Each release is scored on a 50-item golden Q&A set covering policy, architecture, and operational queries. We report retrieval recall@5, LLM-judge faithfulness, relevance, p50 and p95 latency, and average cost per query. Regressions of more than 5 percent block the release.

Judge configuration. Faithfulness and relevance are graded by a separate LLM that sees only the question, the answer, and the retrieved context. The judge returns structured JSON with scores in [0, 1]. A smaller distilled model is used in CI to keep eval costs low; a larger model is used for pre-release gates.

Guardrails design. Confidence is a weighted blend of retrieval similarity (70 percent) and LLM self-check (30 percent). If confidence is below the min_confidence_threshold, the response is replaced with either an insufficient-information message or a clarifying prompt that lists the top retrieved document IDs.

Observability. All requests emit structured JSON logs via structlog, with a trace_id propagated from the request through retrieval, generation, evaluation, and guardrails. When RAG_DEBUG is true, the prompt preview is included in the response trace; otherwise it is stripped to avoid PII exposure.

Provider support. The service supports OpenAI (gpt-4o-mini, text-embedding-3-small), Anthropic (claude-3-5-haiku) for chat and judge, and a fully local stack using Ollama for chat and sentence-transformers for embeddings. Provider selection is driven by LLM_PROVIDER and EMBEDDER_PROVIDER environment variables.
